chunks.text:
# Business Glossary - E-Commerce Domain (Simplified)

This document defines the core business concepts for the e-commerce platform test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase.

**Business Context**: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, an... [TRUNCATED]

**Business Context**: Line items break down orders into individual product purchases. Each captures the price at the moment of ordering.

**Key Attributes**:
- Unique line identifier
- References exactly one Product
- Quantity of units ordered
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current)
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price

**Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item
- Cannot be modified after order confirmation
- Unit price is fixed and does not chan... [TRUNCATED]

Sales Order Relationships:
- A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer
- A Sales Order has one or more Order Line Items
- A Sales Order has one or more Payments
- A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

Order Line Item Relationships:
- An Order Line Item is part of exactly one Sales Order
- An Order Line Item references exactly one Product

Payment Relationships:
- A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order

Shipment Relationships:
- A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order
- A Shipment co... [TRUNCATED]

## MODIFIED FOR TEST A
Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

## SECOND MODIFICATION — extra term
Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund.

# Data Dictionary - E-Commerce Database (Simplified)

This document describes the physical database schema for the simplified e-commerce test dataset.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-----------... [TRUNCATED]

**Foreign Keys**:
- CUST_ID → CUSTOMER_MASTER(CUST_ID)

**Business Concept Mapping**: SalesOrder

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| LINE_ID | INT | Unique line identifier | NO... [TRUNCATED]

CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID

TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID

TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference)

SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID

BUSINESS CONCEPT TO TABLE MAPPING
═════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder     ... [TRUNCATED]

entities_post_resolution.name: Active/Inactive Status
entities_post_resolution.definition: A binary attribute indicating whether an item is currently active or inactive.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Key Attributes:
- Active/inactive status | Key Attributes:
- Status: PENDING, CONFIRMED, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Category
entities_post_resolution.definition: Represents the product category dimension, i.e., the set of category records in TB_CATEGORY that classify products and are referenced by TB_PRODUCT via CATEGORY_ID.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Category reference | NO |
| UNIT_PRICE | DECIMAL(10,2) | Price in EUR | NO |

**Foreign Keys**:
- CATEGORY_ID → TB_CATEGORY(CATEGORY_ID)
 | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | TB_CATEGORY
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Hierarchical product category tree.

**Primary Key**: CATEGORY_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Unique category identifier | NO |
| CATEGORY_NAME | VARCHAR(200) | Category display name | NO |
| PARENT_CATEGORY_ID | INT | Parent category for hierarchy | YES |
 | TB_CATEGORY.CATEGORY_ID
  ├── TB_PRODUCT.CATEGORY_ID
  └── TB_CATEGORY.PARENT_CATEGORY_ID (self-reference) | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer Master(Cust Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: A foreign key constraint linking the SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID column to the CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID primary key, thereby associating each order with its customer master record.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
SALES_ORDER_HDR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Header record for each customer sales order.

**Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment)

**Column Details**:
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO |
| CUST_ID | INT | Customer who placed order | NO |
| ORDER_DATE | DATE | Date order was placed | NO |
| TOTAL_AMT | DECIMAL(12,2) | Total order value | NO |
| STATUS_CODE | VARCHAR(20) | PENDING/CONFIRMED/SHIPPED/DELIVERED/CANCELLED | NO |
| PAYMENT_CONFIRMED_AT | DATETIME | Payment confirmation timestamp | YES |
| DELIVERED_AT | DATETIME | Delivery timestamp | YES |
| CREATED_AT | DATETIME | Order creation timestamp | NO |
 | CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Foreign Keys**:
-

entities_post_resolution.name: Customer
entities_post_resolution.definition: A registered platform user who has made at least one purchase.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Definition: An individual or entity registered on the platform who has made at least one purchase. | Business Context: Customers are the primary actors in the e-commerce system. They browse products, place orders, and receive shipments. | Key Attributes:
- Unique Customer ID | Key Attributes:
- Valid email address (required for login) | Key Attributes:
- One or more delivery/billing addresses | Key Attributes:
- Geographic region for tax calculation | Key Attributes:
- Active/inactive status | Business Rules:
- Must place at least one order to remain active | Business Rules:
- Can have multiple addresses but only one primary per type | Business Rules:
- Data retained for 7 years after last purchase | A Sales Order is placed by exactly one Customer | CUSTOMER_MASTER
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master record for all registered platform customers.

**Primary Key**: CUST_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product

entities_post_resolution.name: Shipment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Shipment is the business entity representing a unit of goods tied to a sales order, identified by a unique shipment ID, and tracked through statuses such as PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, and DELIVERED.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | **Key Attributes**:
- Status: PREPARING, SHIPPED, IN_TRANSIT, DELIVERED | A Shipment is for exactly one Sales Order | A Shipment comes from exactly one Warehouse | A Shipment goes to exactly one Customer Address | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR
OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM
Category         → TB_CATEGORY
Payment          → PAYMENT
Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: SHIPMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | A Sales Order has one or more Shipments

entities_post_resolution.name: Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: A line item is a single entry on a sales order or invoice that references exactly one product and specifies its quantity and price.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Product | **Examples**:
- Line 1: 2 units of Wireless Headphones at €79.99 each | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Business Rules**:
- A Sales Order must contain at least one line item

entities_post_resolution.name: Order Line Item
entities_post_resolution.definition: An individual product line within a Sales Order representing a single product, its quantity, and its price.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Business Rules:
- Must contain at least one Order Line Item | ORDER LINE ITEM
----------------────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: A single line within a Sales Order specifying a Product, quantity, and unit price at time of purchase. | Key Attributes:
- References exactly one Product | Key Attributes:
- Quantity of units ordered | Key Attributes:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | Key Attributes:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | ORDER_LINE_ITEM
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Individual product lines within a sales order.

**Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | **Primary Key**: LINE_ID (INT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID)
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | TB_PRODUCT.PRODUCT_ID
  └── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.PRODUCT_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | Customer

entities_post_resolution.name: Payment
entities_post_resolution.definition: Payment is a financial transaction record linked to a Sales Order, identified by a unique PAYMENT_ID, and an order may have one or more payments.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Sales Order | A Payment is for exactly one Sales Order | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR
OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM
Category         → TB_CATEGORY
Payment          → PAYMENT
Shipment         → SHIPMENT | **Primary Key**: PAYMENT_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | A Sales Order has one or more Payments

entities_post_resolution.name: Product
entities_post_resolution.definition: Product: a sellable item in the catalog, identified by a unique product_id, with a display name, SKU, category, and price.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Key Attributes:
- Unique SKU code | Key Attributes:
- Unit price in base currency (EUR) | Key Attributes:
- Human-readable product name | Key Attributes:
- Active/inactive status | Business Rules:
- Must belong to a valid category before being sold | **Key Attributes**:
- References exactly one Product | An Order Line Item references exactly one Product | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR
OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM
Category         → TB_CATEGORY
Payment          → PAYMENT
Shipment         → SHIPMENT | PRODUCT
----------------────────────────────────────────────────────
**Definition**: A tangible or digital item offered for sale on the platform. | Key Attributes:
- Belongs to exactly one Category | "Definition": A tangible or digital item offered for sale on the platform. | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

entities_post_resolution.name: Sales Order Hdr(Order Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: Sales order header table for customer orders, with one row per order and ORDER_ID as the primary key, used as the parent in related order-item and product relationships.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID)
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | **Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID) | SALES_ORDER_HDR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Header record for each customer sales order.

**Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment)
 | SALES_ORDER_HDR
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Header record for each customer sales order.

**Primary Key**: ORDER_ID (BIGINT, auto-increment)

**Column Details":
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| ORDER_ID | BIGINT | Unique order identifier | NO |
 | CUSTOMER_MASTER.CUST_ID
  └── SALES_ORDER_HDR.CUST_ID | SALES_ORDER_HDR.ORDER_ID
  ├── ORDER_LINE_ITEM.ORDER_ID
  ├── PAYMENT.ORDER_ID
  └── SHIPMENT.ORDER_ID | Customer         → CUSTOMER_MASTER
Product          → TB_PRODUCT
SalesOrder       → SALES_ORDER_HDR
OrderLineItem    → ORDER_LINE_ITEM
C

entities_post_resolution.name: Tb Product(Product Id)
entities_post_resolution.definition: The PRODUCT_ID column of the TB_PRODUCT table, serving as the primary key that uniquely identifies each product and is referenced by foreign keys from other tables.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
**Foreign Keys**:
- ORDER_ID → SALES_ORDER_HDR(ORDER_ID)
- PRODUCT_ID → TB_PRODUCT(PRODUCT_ID) | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

**Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment)
 | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

**Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details":
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| PRODUCT_ID | INT | Unique product identifier | NO |
| SKU | VARCHAR(50) | Stock Keeping Unit code | NO |
 | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

**Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details":
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|

entities_post_resolution.name: Unit Price At
entities_post_resolution.definition: The per-unit price of a product at the moment the order was placed (historical unit price used for line-item calculation).
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text:
Key Attributes:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | Key Attributes:
- Quantity of units ordered | TB_PRODUCT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
**Description**: Master catalogue of all products available for sale.

**Primary Key**: PRODUCT_ID (INT, auto-increment)

**Column Details":
| Column | Type | Description | Nullable |
|--------|------|-------------|----------|
| PRODUCT_ID | INT | Unique product identifier | NO |
| SKU | VARCHAR(50) | Stock Keeping Unit code | NO |
| PRODUCT_NAME | VARCHAR(300) | Product display name | NO |
| CATEGORY_ID | INT | Category reference | NO |
| UNIT_PRICE | DECIMAL(10,2) | Price in EUR | NO |
 | **Business Rules**:
- Unit price is fixed and does not change with future price updates | Key Attributes:
- Unit price in base currency (EUR) | **Key Attributes**:
- Unit price at time of order (historical, not current) | **Key Attributes**:
- Extended amount = quantity × unit price | **Examples**:
- Line 1: 2

entities_post_resolution.name: Return Policy
entities_post_resolution.definition: Return Policy: a business policy that specifies the terms under which customers may return products, typically within a set period, for a refund.
entities_post_resolution.provenance_text: Return Policy: Products can be returned within 30 days of purchase for a full refund. | Refund: A reimbursement issued to a customer when an order is cancelled.

entities_post_resolution.definition:

tables_enriched.name: Customer Master
tables_enriched.description: Stores core customer information including identifiers, contact details, regional segmentation, account creation timestamp, and active status.

tables_enriched.name: Product
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about products, including identifiers, naming, category association, pricing, and whether the product is currently active.

tables_enriched.name: Category
tables_enriched.description: Stores hierarchical category data for products, including each category's name and a reference to its parent category for categorization and navigation.

tables_enriched.name: Sales Order Header
tables_enriched.description: Stores header information for sales orders, including customer reference, order date, total amount, status, payment confirmation, delivery timestamp, and record creation time.

tables_enriched.name: Order Line Item
tables_enriched.description: Stores line-by-line details of each order, including which product was ordered, the quantity, unit price, and the total amount for the line.

tables_enriched.name: Payment
tables_enriched.description: Stores information about payments applied to customer orders, including method, amount, status, and timestamps for creation and confirmation.

tables_enriched.name: Shipment
tables_enriched.description: Stores records of shipments, including the association to customer orders, the warehouse dispatch location, shipment timestamp, tracking identifier, and current shipment status.
